title: High-Protein Chicken Breast Plate
tagline: Spiced curd-marinated chicken breast with masala potato wedges and charred carrots — or rolled into a high-protein paratha kathi roll.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients — the biggest swing is oil, so weigh it (1 tbsp oil is ~120 kcal and easy to over-pour).

This is a ~720 kcal/serving plate. To cut for a lean day: drop the potato to 150 g/serving, use spray oil instead of poured, and use hung curd not full-fat in the marinade — gets it closer to 550 kcal while keeping ~60 g protein.
Already high protein from the breast. To push further use egg whites in a bhurji variant, or add a second smaller breast.

items: 500 g chicken breast (2 large), butterflied or lightly pounded even
200 g thick curd or hung curd (or Greek yogurt)
500 g potatoes (4 medium), cut into wedges
Pinch chaat masala after cooking, optional
300 g carrots, cut into thick batons
Mint hung-curd sauce (hung curd + mint + roasted cumin + salt)
Sliced onion + lemon + pinch chaat masala

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
steps.body: Butterfly 500 g chicken breast into even, thinner pieces (or pound flat) so it cooks evenly and stays juicy. Coat in 200 g curd + 2 tsp ginger-garlic paste + 2 tsp lemon + 2 tsp Kashmiri chilli + 1 tsp turmeric + 2 tsp coriander powder + 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp garam masala + 1.5 tsp salt + 1 tsp pepper + 2 tsp mustard oil. Marinate at least 30 minutes, ideally 2-4 hours.

steps.title: Parboil the potatoes
steps.body: Cut 500 g potatoes into wedges and boil in salted water for 6-8 minutes, only until barely tender. Drain and let them steam-dry 5 minutes — this is what gives crispy outside, fluffy inside.

steps.title: Season the wedges
steps.body: Toss the dried wedges with 2 tsp oil + 1 tsp chilli + 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp coriander powder + 1/2 tsp turmeric + 1 tsp garlic powder + salt + pepper.

steps.title: Prep the carrots
steps.body: Toss 300 g carrot batons with a little oil, salt and pepper.

steps.title: Oven roast
steps.body: Preheat oven to 220°C. Spread wedges, carrots and chicken on a tray with space between them (don't pile). Roast the potatoes and carrots 30-40 minutes, flipping halfway. Add the thin chicken pieces for their last 18-22 minutes, until internal temp is 74°C (or the thickest part runs clear when cut). Rest the chicken 5 minutes before slicing.

steps.title: No-oven pan method
steps.body: Chicken: shake off excess marinade, cook in a little oil on medium 5-7 min first side, 4-6 min second, then cover on low 2 min if needed; rest before slicing. Potatoes: wide pan, 1-1.5 tsp oil, covered on medium 5 min then uncovered until crisp. Carrots: 1 tsp butter + a pinch of cumin seeds, medium-high until charred and softened, finish with a squeeze of lemon.

steps.title: Serve as a plate
steps.body: Plate the sliced chicken with the masala wedges and carrots. Sprinkle a pinch of chaat masala on the wedges and finish the carrots with lemon and a pinch of chilli flakes.

steps.title: OR serve as a paratha wrap
steps.body: Skip the wedges/carrots — pile the sliced chicken onto a warm high-protein paratha with sliced onion, lemon, chaat masala and a spoon of mint hung-curd sauce, then roll it into a kathi roll.

notes: Flavour upgrades (pick one): a green chutney drizzle (coriander + mint + green chilli + lemon + curd) is insane with the chicken; a pinch of kasuri methi + smoked paprika in the marinade gives a dhaba vibe; chilli flakes + garlic + lemon zest on the wedges makes a peri-peri-ish version.
The paratha route also works as a chicken bhurji or keema plate — dry-ish spiced chicken scooped with paratha and curd + onion.
Keep chicken pieces thin and even; thick whole breast on high heat dries out.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
